perturbation.original_text: This Agreement controls Confidential Information which is disclosed from the Effective Date for a period of three (3) years at which time the Agreement will terminate unless extended in writing by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement controls Confidential Information which is disclosed from the Effective Date for a period of one (1) year at which time the Agreement will terminate unless extended in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement controls confidential information for three years from the effective date. By changing this to one year, it creates an in-text contradiction concerning the duration of the agreement's control over confidential information. This ambiguity affects the enforceability of the confidentiality clauses and could lead to disputes regarding the protection of disclosed information after one year but before three years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Period and Expiration. This Agreement controls Confidential Information which is disclosed from the Effective Date for a period of one (1) year at which time the Agreement will terminate unless extended in writing by both parties. The Receiving Party’s duty of non-disclosure under this Agreement shall extend beyond the term of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of last disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a three-year period of control over confidential information. The changed text shortens this period to one year. The contradicted text states, 'This Agreement controls Confidential Information which is disclosed from the Effective Date for a period of one (1) year'. Because the changed text now matches the contradicted text, the change created a contradiction based on the perturbation explanation. The original text would have kept the agreement in effect for 3 years, while the changed text would only keep it in effect for 1 year. Thus, the contradicted text would be in disagreement with the original text, while agreement with the changed text. Therefore, a contradiction exists due to the change, as the protection period for confidential information is now inconsistent with what it originally was.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the state courts of Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that the state courts of Travis County, Texas and the federal courts located in the Western District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Switching the jurisdiction and venue from Collin or Dallas Counties (Eastern District) to Travis County (Western District) creates a direct contradiction. This makes it unclear which courts have the authority to resolve disputes, potentially leading to legal challenges and increased costs associated with determining the proper forum. It creates an in-text contradiction on the enforcement of the dispute resolution process. This affects enforceability as the agreement now contains conflicting terms on the same subject matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that the state courts of Travis County, Texas and the federal courts located in the Western District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the exclusive jurisdiction and venue from Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas (Eastern District) to Travis County, Texas (Western District). The contradicted text also states that the state courts of Travis County, Texas and the federal courts located in the Western District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction. The initial change created a direct contradiction with the contradicted text because they cannot both be exclusively correct. The changed text contradicts the contradicted text by having different jurisdictions that are meant to be exclusive.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees not to publicize or disclose the existence or terms of this Agreement to any third party without the prior consent of the other party except as required by law (in which case, the party seeking to disclose the information shall give reasonable notice to the other party of its intent to make such a disclosure).
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees not to publicize or disclose the existence or terms of this Agreement to any third party without the prior consent of the other party except as required by law (in which case, the party seeking to disclose the information shall give 24 hours notice to the other party of its intent to make such a disclosure).
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires 'reasonable notice' for disclosing the agreement's existence or terms when required by law. Changing this to '24 hours notice' creates an in-text contradiction. What constitutes 'reasonable' can be subjective and depend on circumstances, whereas '24 hours' is a specific timeframe. This creates ambiguity, as a 24-hour notice might not always be considered reasonable in complex legal situations. In some legal circumstances 24 hours might be too late to react. This creates contradictory terms in the same section regarding how notice is properly given and when it is considered reasonable. This affects enforceability as the agreement now contains conflicting terms on the same subject matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Protect/Publicity. The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. The Receiving Party (i) shall limit access to all Confidential Information to its employees, agents, representatives, consultants and contractors who shall reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above and to third party vendors for the purpose of obtaining price quotations, (ii) shall use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the Purpose, and (iii) shall use due diligence to insure that all such persons are aware of, understand their obligations under, and comply with the provisions of this Agreement. Each party agrees not to publicize or disclose the existence or terms of this Agreement to any third party without the prior consent of the other party except as required by law (in which case, the party seeking to disclose the information shall give 24 hours notice to the other party of its intent to make such a disclosure). Neither party shall make any press release or similar public statement without the prior consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for disclosing agreement terms when legally required, changing it from 'reasonable notice' to '24 hours notice'. The contradicted text includes a similar clause within the 'Duty to Protect/Publicity' section, which was also modified in the same way, replacing the original 'reasonable notice' with '24 hours notice'. This replacement creates a contradiction because 'reasonable notice' is subjective and context-dependent, while '24 hours' is a specific timeframe that might not always constitute 'reasonable notice' under all circumstances, creating uncertainty and conflicting terms within the same section regarding how notice is properly given and when it is considered reasonable. The Perturbation Explanation details that in some circumstances a 24-hour notice might be too late to react. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as the sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations or deadlines.
